sections.paragraph: ἀποδημέω [ἀπόδημος] ‘be away / go away from one’s locality’, go on a trip, take a trip, leave Mt 21:33; 25:14f; Mk 12:1; Lk 15:13; 20:9. In ext. sense and in wordplay, of St. Paul who views himself as a temporary resident in his body but away from the Lord, i.e. he is homesick for the Lord’s presence 2 Cor 5:6 v.l.
